title: AI Lab Journey
subtitle: Telkom University AI Laboratory
reflection: Teaching weekly required understanding each concept well enough to handle any question on the spot.
Recruiting taught me what matters in a research context: track record, not just potential.
A coordinator's responsibilities are different from a member's. Members execute. Coordinators ensure others can execute consistently.
Supervising project groups showed me how many ways a project can be technically functional and scientifically weak at the same time.
Evaluating research quality means asking whether the question was worth answering, not just whether the code ran.

highlights: First year in AI Lab. Joined as a member, assigned as team lead on the plant disease classifier project.
Built the full pipeline in Python: data cleaning, training DL model for computer vision, Streamlit deployment. Completed in one semester.
Won 3rd place at CCI Summit Kaggle competition the same year, first competition as team lead.
Awarded 2nd Best Final Project in the Study Group.

milestones.role: Study Group Coordinator · Focused Group Member
highlights: Became Study Group Coordinator: recruited, taught, and evaluated 29 students across weekly sessions and a final project.
Supervised 15 project groups from proposal to final demonstration. Provided guidance during development, then judged and awarded at final presentations.
Joined the Focused Group and led the multimodal deepfake detection project: a model handling both video and audio modalities with explainability output.
Appointed as Focused Group Coordinator, overseeing research-active members within AI Lab.
Setting research direction, reviewing member output, and connecting projects to industry and academic partners.
Current projects include medical imaging, NLP, and computer vision research tracks.
